title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for playtest closure, release gates, rollback, recovery, and production defense and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for production game release and defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Playtest Closure · changed case · Quiz Playtest Closure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 7482 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

Accept the Production Defense result because visible production game release and defense dossier output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Production Defense.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Production Defense for production game release and defense dossier.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Playtest Closure
steps.instruction:
Order the Playtest Closure Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Playtest Closure.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Playtest Closure assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Playtest Closure, keep Playtest Closure connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9292 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Production Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 5149 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Production Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Production Defense through the concrete production game release and defense dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.
